title: Narcissus micranthus
taxonomy: Narcissus micranthus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Narcissus, which belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae. The genus Narcissus is further divided into several sections, with Narcissus micranthus being a member of the section Jonquillae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flowers and foliage. The Amaryllidaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as amaryllis or bellflowers. Within the family, the Narcissus genus is one of the largest and most diverse groups.
uses: Narcissus micranthus has been traditionally used in various forms of medicine in South Africa, where it is native. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. In traditional medicine, the bulbs of Narcissus micranthus are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are used in teas and infusions to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of indigestion. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Narcissus micranthus as a medicinal herb.

preparations: Narcissus micranthus is a species of plant that is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in South Africa where it is native. intro: Narcissus micranthus, also known as the small-flowered daffodil, is a bulbous perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its small, white or yellowish flowers and strap-like leaves. Traditionally, Narcissus micranthus has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and colds. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus micranthus include alkaloids, saponins, and other compounds, such as galantamine, which has been shown to possess acetylcholinesterase inhibitory activity, and narcissine, a toxic alkaloid with potential antimicrobial properties. In herbal medicine, Narcissus micranthus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues and other conditions.
